title: WTO Sets Up Dispute Panel Over India's Solar, IT Goods Measures
summary: China has successfully established a WTO panel to challenge India's solar cell, module, and IT goods policies, alleging unfair targeting of Chinese products.
story_fact: WTO sets up dispute panel on China's case against India's solar, IT goods measures
background: The WTO Dispute Settlement Body is a mechanism for resolving trade disputes between member countries. The Dispute Settlement Understanding (DSU) sets out the rules and procedures for dispute settlement.
prelims: The WTO Dispute Settlement Body has the authority to establish panels to resolve trade disputes between member countries.
China's complaint against India's solar and IT goods policies alleges violations of Article XX of the GATT 1994.
The dispute panel will examine India's trade commitments regarding solar cells, modules, and IT goods, including tariffs and incentives.
The WTO Agreement on Subsidies and Countervailing Measures is relevant to this dispute, as China alleges that India's policies unfairly target Chinese products.
The dispute panel's decision will be based on the WTO Dispute Settlement Understanding (DSU) and relevant WTO agreements.

title: CII President R Mukundan Projects 7% Growth
summary: CII President R Mukundan projects 7% growth for India, seeks GST Council-like body to drive reforms across sectors like agriculture and land.
story_fact: CII President R Mukundan projects 7% growth for India this fiscal year.

prelims: The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) is a non-governmental trade association and advocacy group in India, with over 9,000 members.

India's GDP growth rate has been projected to be around 7% this fiscal year, with most businesses finding growth avenues despite global cost pressures.

Statement 3 is incorrect: There is no information in the story about the proposed GST Council-like body having the power to acquire land for industrial purposes.

title: Govt draws up contingency plans for 315 districts amid 42% rain deficit
summary: India faces a 42% rainfall deficit this monsoon, exacerbated by El Nino. The government is implementing contingency plans across 315 vulnerable districts.
story_fact: India faces a 42% rainfall deficit this monsoon.
background: The Indian monsoon season typically runs from June to September. El Nino is a climate pattern that can impact monsoon rainfall.
prelims: The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) tracks monsoon progress and issues forecasts. The 42% deficit is based on data up to June 20.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) oversees disaster response, including droughts. It works with state governments to implement contingency plans.
The government has identified 315 districts as vulnerable to the monsoon deficit. These districts will receive priority support.
The Ministry of Agriculture has advised farmers on crop diversification and water conservation. This includes promoting drought-resistant crop varieties.
The El Nino-Southern Oscillation (ENSO) cycle can impact Indian monsoon patterns. ENSO is being closely monitored for its potential impact on the ongoing monsoon season.

mains.q: The monsoon deficit and El Nino phenomenon raise questions about India's agricultural resilience. Examine the role of crop diversification and water conservation in mitigating the impacts of such climate-related stresses.

3. Crop insurance schemes are a primary tool for mitigating agricultural losses due to monsoon deficits.

_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: while El Nino can impact monsoon patterns, the relationship is complex and not always straightforward. Statement 2 is correct: the government has indeed focused on water conservation. Statement 3 is correct: crop insurance is a key mechanism for supporting farmers affected by droughts.

_mcqs.q:

title: India-UK FTA Set to Take Effect on July 15
summary: The India-UK trade pact promises to boost bilateral ties and open a market exceeding $500 billion for Indian businesses, with the agreement set to take effect on July 15.
story_fact: India-UK FTA to take effect on July 15, opening a $500 billion market
background: The India-UK Free Trade Agreement aims to reduce trade barriers and increase economic cooperation between the two nations. The agreement has been negotiated to promote mutual trade and investment.
prelims: The India-UK FTA is expected to increase bilateral trade by reducing tariffs and other trade barriers, with a focus on sectors like textiles and pharmaceuticals.
The Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) has been a key aspect of India-UK trade negotiations, with the UK seeking to maintain preferential access to the Indian market.
The UK's withdrawal from the EU has led to a need for new trade agreements, including the India-UK FTA, which aims to promote economic cooperation and reduce trade barriers.
The WTO's Most-Favoured-Nation (MFN) principle requires that trading partners extend the same trade concessions to all WTO members, which could impact the India-UK FTA negotiations.
The Indian government's 'Make in India' initiative aims to promote domestic manufacturing and attract foreign investment, which could be supported by the India-UK FTA.

mains.q: The India-UK FTA's potential impact on Indian industries, particularly textiles and pharmaceuticals, has been a subject of debate. Examine the trade-offs involved in this agreement and suggest measures to mitigate potential negative consequences.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: increased market access for Indian exporters versus potential job losses in domestic industries due to cheaper UK imports.
Cite the example of the EU-India FTA negotiations, which stalled due to disagreements over tariffs and market access, and compare the India-UK FTA's approach to addressing these issues.
Distinguish between the 'Most-Favoured-Nation' (MFN) principle and the 'National Treatment' principle in the context of the India-UK FTA, and discuss their implications for Indian industries.

Apply the concept of 'comparative advantage' to argue that the India-UK FTA could lead to increased efficiency and competitiveness in Indian industries, particularly in sectors like textiles and pharmaceuticals.

Conclude by proposing a phased implementation of the FTA, with regular reviews and assessments to mitigate potential negative consequences and ensure that the agreement benefits both Indian and UK industries.

connect.context: The India-UK FTA's tariff reductions may be subject to TRQs, which could impact the agreement's effectiveness in promoting trade between the two nations.
The India-UK FTA negotiations may be influenced by the EU-India FTA negotiations, which have stalled due to disagreements over tariffs and market access.
The India-UK FTA may be compared to the US-UK trade agreement, which has been negotiated to promote trade and investment between the two nations.

connect.topic: Brexit's Impact on UK Trade Policy
connect.context: The India-UK FTA may be influenced by Brexit's impact on UK trade policy, which has led to a need for new trade agreements and a re-evaluation of the UK's trade relationships with other nations.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The India-UK FTA aims to reduce trade barriers and increase economic cooperation between the two nations.
2. The FTA will lead to a significant increase in Indian exports to the UK.
3. The agreement will result in a substantial reduction in Indian imports from the UK.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the India-UK FTA aims to promote mutual trade and investment. Statement 2 is correct: the FTA is expected to increase Indian exports to the UK. Statement 3 is incorrect: the agreement may not necessarily lead to a substantial reduction in Indian imports from the UK.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The India-UK FTA will come into effect on July 15, 2026.
2. The agreement will provide duty-free access to the UK market for all Indian exports.
3. The FTA will require the UK to maintain its current level of tariffs on Indian imports.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the India-UK FTA is set to take effect on July 15, 2026. Statement 2 is incorrect: the agreement may not provide duty-free access to the UK market for all Indian exports. Statement 3 is incorrect: the FTA may not require the UK to maintain its current level of tariffs on Indian imports.

title: EAC-PM Chairman: 7-8% Growth Needed for Viksit Bharat
summary: India needs 7-8% economic growth to become 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047, driven by private investment and exports, with structural reforms in place.
story_fact: India needs 7-8% growth for Viksit Bharat by 2047.
background: The 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative focuses on enhancing domestic capability and product quality for global competitiveness. The government also prioritizes organic farming to reduce fertilizer subsidies.
prelims: The Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative aims to enhance domestic capability and product quality for global competitiveness, not import substitution.
The Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM) provides advice on economic issues, including growth and development.
India has set a target of becoming Viksit Bharat by 2047, with a focus on sustained economic growth.
The government has implemented structural reforms to drive private investment and exports.
The organic farming initiative aims to reduce fertilizer subsidies and promote sustainable agriculture.

mains.q: The EAC-PM Chairman's statement on 7-8% growth for Viksit Bharat by 2047 highlights the need for sustained economic growth. Examine.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: high growth rates boost economic development but may lead to inflation and resource depletion.
Cite the example of the East Asian Tigers, which achieved high growth rates through export-led growth and investment in human capital.
Distinguish between extensive and intensive growth, and argue which approach is more suitable for India's development.
Apply the concept of inclusive growth, and suggest measures to ensure that the benefits of growth are shared by all sections of society.
Conclude by proposing a policy framework to achieve sustained economic growth and become Viksit Bharat by 2047.

connect.context: The Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative is connected to the Make in India program, which aims to promote domestic manufacturing and enhance global competitiveness.
The Digital India initiative is connected to the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative, as it aims to promote digital literacy and enhance digital infrastructure.
The Skill India initiative is connected to the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative, as it aims to promote vocational training and enhance human capital.
The Swachh Bharat Abhiyan is connected to the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative, as it aims to promote cleanliness and hygiene, and enhance the overall quality of life.
The Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana is connected to the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative, as it aims to promote financial inclusion and enhance access to banking services.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The EAC-PM Chairman's statement on 7-8% growth for Viksit Bharat by 2047 is based on the assumption of high investment in human capital.
2. The Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative focuses on enhancing domestic capability and product quality for global competitiveness.
3. The government's priority on organic farming is aimed at reducing fertilizer subsidies and promoting sustainable agriculture.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the EAC-PM Chairman's statement is based on the assumption of high investment in human capital. Statement 2 is correct: the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative focuses on enhancing domestic capability and product quality for global competitiveness. Statement 3 is correct: the government's priority on organic farming is aimed at reducing fertilizer subsidies and promoting sustainable agriculture.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM) provides advice on economic issues, including growth and development.
2. The EAC-PM is headed by the Prime Minister.
3. The EAC-PM has a fixed tenure of five years.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the EAC-PM provides advice on economic issues, including growth and development. Statement 2 is incorrect: the EAC-PM is headed by a Chairman, not the Prime Minister. Statement 3 is incorrect: the EAC-PM does not have a fixed tenure of five years.

title: India Prepares Contingency Plans Amid 40% Rainfall Deficit
summary: Agriculture Commissioner PK Singh stated that India has district-level contingency plans to tackle possible rainfall uncertainty, with irrigation capacity and food grain reserves providing a buffer against any potential impact on crop production.
story_fact: India faces a 40% rainfall deficit with contingency plans in place.
background: The Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare oversees such contingency planning. Section 19 of related acts defines service voters and other critical aspects.
prelims: The Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare is responsible for overseeing contingency planning for agricultural production, including responding to rainfall deficits.
The Rainfall Deficit of 40% necessitates the implementation of contingency plans to mitigate its impact on crop production, as outlined in the National Disaster Management Plan.
A 40% rainfall deficit has been reported, prompting the activation of district-level contingency plans to ensure food security and minimize economic losses.
The National Food Security Mission aims to increase food production and minimize the impact of rainfall deficits on agricultural output, aligning with the contingency plans.

The district-level contingency plans include strategies for irrigation management, crop insurance, and support for farmers affected by the rainfall deficit, demonstrating a nuanced approach to disaster management.

mains.q: The implementation of district-level contingency plans in response to rainfall deficits raises questions about the effectiveness of India's agricultural risk management strategies. Examine.
hints: Frame the discussion around the trade-offs between irrigation capacity, food grain reserves, and the economic viability of contingency planning.
Cite the example of the 2015-2016 rainfall deficit and how it affected agricultural production, highlighting the lessons learned and applied to current contingency plans.
Distinguish between the roles of the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare and state governments in implementing contingency plans, emphasizing coordination and resource allocation.
Apply the principles of the National Disaster Management Plan to argue for a more proactive approach to agricultural risk management, incorporating climate-resilient practices.
Conclude by proposing a review mechanism for contingency plans, ensuring they remain effective and adaptable to changing climatic conditions and agricultural practices.

connect.context: The plan outlines the framework for disaster management, including contingency planning for agricultural production, highlighting the importance of proactive measures against rainfall deficits.
Such schemes are part of the contingency plans, providing financial support to farmers affected by rainfall deficits, and are critical for agricultural risk management.
Effective irrigation management is crucial for mitigating the impact of rainfall deficits, and contingency plans must include strategies for efficient water use and allocation.
The implementation of contingency plans is a key aspect of agricultural risk management, requiring a comprehensive approach that includes climate-resilient practices and support for farmers.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare is responsible for overseeing contingency planning for agricultural production.
2. A 40% rainfall deficit automatically triggers the implementation of district-level contingency plans.
3. The National Food Security Mission is directly involved in the implementation of these contingency plans.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare oversees contingency planning. Statement 2 is incorrect: The implementation of contingency plans depends on various factors, not just the percentage of rainfall deficit. Statement 3 is incorrect: While the National Food Security Mission aims to increase food production, it is not directly involved in the implementation of contingency plans.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The district-level contingency plans include strategies for irrigation management and crop insurance.
2. These plans are reviewed and updated annually to reflect changing climatic conditions and agricultural practices.
3. The implementation of these plans is solely the responsibility of state governments.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The plans do include such strategies. Statement 2 is correct: Regular review and update are essential for the plans' effectiveness. Statement 3 is incorrect: The implementation involves both state governments and the central government through the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare.

title: RBI Delays Rate Hike to Second Half of FY27
summary: Economists expect the RBI to delay interest rate hikes until the second half of FY27 due to easing inflation risks and expected foreign currency inflows.
story_fact: RBI may delay rate hike to second half of FY27 due to easing inflation risks.
background: The RBI manages monetary policy, including setting interest rates. The central bank aims to control inflation while promoting economic growth.
prelims: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is responsible for managing India's monetary policy, including setting interest rates to control inflation.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is the key body that decides on interest rates, with the aim of keeping inflation within the target range of 2-6%.
The RBI has been monitoring inflation closely, as high inflation can erode the purchasing power of consumers and undermine economic growth.
The Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act, 2003 provides a framework for the government to manage its finances and reduce fiscal deficits.
The foreign currency inflows into India can help reduce the country's trade deficit and support economic growth.

mains.q: The RBI's decision to delay interest rate hikes may have significant implications for India's economic growth. Examine.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: higher interest rates can control inflation but may slow down economic growth.
Cite the example of the 2013 taper tantrum, where the US Federal Reserve's decision to raise interest rates led to a sharp decline in emerging market currencies.
Distinguish between the RBI's monetary policy goals and the government's fiscal policy objectives.
Apply the concept of the Taylor rule, which suggests that interest rates should be set based on inflation and output gaps.
Conclude by discussing the potential risks and benefits of the RBI's decision to delay interest rate hikes.

connect.topic: Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act, 2003
connect.context: The FRBM Act provides a framework for the government to manage its finances and reduce fiscal deficits, which can help support economic growth.
The Taylor rule suggests that interest rates should be set based on inflation and output gaps, which can help guide the RBI's monetary policy decisions.
The FEMA regulates foreign exchange transactions, and its provisions can affect the RBI's ability to manage the exchange rate and control inflation.
The Banking Regulation Act regulates the banking sector, and its provisions can affect the RBI's ability to supervise and regulate banks, which can have an impact on the economy.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI is responsible for managing India's monetary policy.
2. The MPC is responsible for setting interest rates.
3. The FRBM Act provides a framework for the government to manage its finances.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The RBI is responsible for managing India's monetary policy. Statement 2 is correct: The MPC is responsible for setting interest rates. Statement 3 is correct: The FRBM Act provides a framework for the government to manage its finances.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI's decision to delay interest rate hikes is due to high inflation.
2. The RBI's decision to delay interest rate hikes is due to low economic growth.
3. The RBI's decision to delay interest rate hikes is due to expected foreign currency inflows.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The RBI's decision to delay interest rate hikes is not due to high inflation. Statement 2 is incorrect: The RBI's decision to delay interest rate hikes is not due to low economic growth. Statement 3 is correct: The RBI's decision to delay interest rate hikes is due to expected foreign currency inflows.

title: Telangana to Take Over Hyderabad Metro Rail Project
summary: Telangana CM A. Revanth Reddy convinced Union Ministers to support the state's decision to take over the Hyderabad Metro Rail project and propose its expansion, with SBICAPS as consultant.
story_fact: Telangana government to take over Hyderabad Metro Rail project with SBICAPS as consultant.
background: The Hyderabad Metro Rail project is a public-private partnership. The Telangana government's takeover is aimed at expanding the project.
prelims: The Hyderabad Metro Rail project is a joint venture between the Telangana government and a private consortium, with a total length of 72 km.
The Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model is used for the Hyderabad Metro Rail project, which allows for private sector participation in infrastructure development.
The Telangana government has decided to take over the Hyderabad Metro Rail project, with SBICAPS acting as consultant for the expansion plans.
The Expansion plans for the Hyderabad Metro Rail project include adding new lines and stations, which will increase the project's total length and coverage area.
The takeover of the Hyderabad Metro Rail project by the Telangana government is expected to improve the project's efficiency and effectiveness, with SBICAPS providing expertise as consultant.

mains.q: The takeover of the Hyderabad Metro Rail project by the Telangana government raises questions about the role of public-private partnerships in infrastructure development. Examine.
hints: Frame the central issue: the trade-off between public and private sector involvement in infrastructure projects, with reference to the Hyderabad Metro Rail project.
Cite the example of the Delhi Metro Rail project, which has been successful in terms of ridership and revenue, and compare it with the Hyderabad Metro Rail project.
Discuss the potential benefits of public-private partnerships in infrastructure development, including increased efficiency and reduced costs, with reference to the Hyderabad Metro Rail project.

Examine the potential risks and challenges associated with public-private partnerships, including the risk of private sector withdrawal and the challenge of ensuring public accountability, with reference to the Hyderabad Metro Rail project.
Conclude by suggesting ways to balance the role of public and private sectors in infrastructure development, with reference to the Hyderabad Metro Rail project and the role of SBICAPS as consultant.

connect.context: The Hyderabad Metro Rail project is an example of a PPP in infrastructure development, which raises questions about the role of public and private sectors in such projects.

The Metro Rail Policy, 2017, provides a framework for the development of metro rail projects in India, including the Hyderabad Metro Rail project, and highlights the importance of PPPs in such projects.

connect.topic: Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act, 2013
connect.context: The Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act, 2013, is relevant to the Hyderabad Metro Rail project, as it provides a framework for land acquisition and rehabilitation in infrastructure projects.
The National Urban Transport Policy, 2006, emphasizes the importance of integrated urban transport systems, including metro rail projects like the Hyderabad Metro Rail project.

The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) is a successful example of a metro rail project in India, which can be compared with the Hyderabad Metro Rail project to identify best practices and challenges.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Hyderabad Metro Rail project is a public-private partnership.
2. The Telangana government has decided to take over the project.
3. SBICAPS is acting as consultant for the expansion plans.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Hyderabad Metro Rail project is a joint venture between the Telangana government and a private consortium. Statement 2 is correct: The Telangana government has decided to take over the project. Statement 3 is correct: SBICAPS is acting as consultant for the expansion plans.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Hyderabad Metro Rail project is the longest metro rail project in India.
2. The project is being expanded to increase its total length and coverage area.
3. The Telangana government has decided to privatize the project.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Hyderabad Metro Rail project is not the longest metro rail project in India. Statement 2 is correct: The project is being expanded to increase its total length and coverage area. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Telangana government has decided to take over the project, not privatize it.

title: Axis AMC's Shreyash Devalkar Invests in Mid and Small Caps
summary: Axis AMC's Shreyash Devalkar favors mid and small caps for structural growth despite elevated valuations, citing AI-driven FII exits and healthy earnings growth in the Indian equity market.
story_fact: Axis AMC's Shreyash Devalkar favors mid and small caps for growth.
background: The Indian equity market has shown resilience with healthy earnings growth. Axis AMC is a leading asset management company in India.
prelims: Axis AMC has invested in mid and small caps due to their growth potential, despite high valuations. The Indian equity market has seen significant FII outflows.
The Indian equity market has shown resilience with healthy earnings growth, driven by strong corporate performance. This trend is expected to continue.
The mid and small cap segment has seen significant growth in recent times, driven by structural themes such as digitalization and financial inclusion.
The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has implemented various measures to promote transparency and accountability in the Indian equity market.
The Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) has been a popular investment option for retail investors, allowing them to invest a fixed amount of money at regular intervals.

mains.q: The Indian equity market has shown resilience despite muted index returns and FII outflows. Examine the factors contributing to this trend. (15 marks · GS3)
hints: Frame the central trade-off: the Indian equity market has shown resilience due to strong corporate earnings and economic growth.
Cite the example of the pharmaceutical sector, which has seen significant growth in recent times due to its strong export potential.
Distinguish between the performance of large caps and mid and small caps, and argue that the latter have more growth potential.
Apply the concept of systematic risk and explain how it affects the Indian equity market.
Conclude by suggesting policy measures to promote transparency and accountability in the Indian equity market.

connect.context: The pharmaceutical sector has seen significant growth in recent times due to its strong export potential, which has contributed to the Indian equity market's resilience.
The Indian equity market has shown resilience due to strong corporate earnings and economic growth, which has been driven by various factors such as government policies and demographic trends.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. Axis AMC has invested in mid and small caps due to their growth potential.
2. The Indian equity market has seen significant FII outflows.
3. The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has implemented measures to promote transparency and accountability in the Indian equity market.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Axis AMC has invested in mid and small caps due to their growth potential. Statement 2 is correct: The Indian equity market has seen significant FII outflows. Statement 3 is correct: SEBI has implemented measures to promote transparency and accountability in the Indian equity market.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Indian equity market has shown resilience due to strong corporate earnings and economic growth.
2. The mid and small cap segment has seen significant growth in recent times.
3. The Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) has been a popular investment option for retail investors.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Indian equity market has shown resilience due to strong corporate earnings and economic growth. Statement 2 is correct: The mid and small cap segment has seen significant growth in recent times. Statement 3 is correct: The Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) has been a popular investment option for retail investors.

title: RBI Governor Urges Banks to Treat MSMEs as Growth Partners
summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra urged banks to view MSMEs as vital partners, investing in technology and innovation for entrepreneurs, leveraging digital infrastructure like Account Aggregator and ULI for efficient credit.
story_fact: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra asked banks to treat MSMEs as growth partners under Section 19.
background: The RBI has been promoting digital transformation in the banking sector. MSMEs are a crucial part of the Indian economy, contributing to GDP and employment.
prelims: The RBI is promoting digital transformation in the banking sector to support MSMEs, with a focus on technology and innovation for entrepreneurs.
The Account Aggregator framework enables secure data sharing, allowing lenders to access borrowers' financial data for efficient credit assessment.
The ULI system is used for unique identification of loan accounts, facilitating credit monitoring and reducing fraud.
The MSME sector contributes significantly to India's GDP and employment, with the government launching various schemes to support its growth.
The digital infrastructure for MSMEs includes initiatives like the Account Aggregator and ULI, aimed at improving access to credit and reducing transaction costs.

mains.q: The RBI's emphasis on treating MSMEs as growth partners raises questions about the role of banks in promoting economic development. Examine.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: banks' risk appetite for MSME lending versus the potential for job creation and GDP growth.
Cite the RBI's recent guidelines on MSME lending, highlighting the focus on digital transformation and data-driven credit assessment.
Distinguish between the role of banks and non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) in supporting MSMEs, discussing the regulatory framework and implications for credit access.
Apply the concept of financial inclusion, discussing how the RBI's initiatives aim to expand access to credit for MSMEs and reduce transaction costs.
Conclude by proposing policy reforms to strengthen the MSME sector, including measures to enhance digital infrastructure and improve credit monitoring.

connect.context: The RBI's initiative to treat MSMEs as growth partners is linked to the priority sector lending framework, which aims to increase credit flow to priority sectors.
The RBI's digital transformation initiative for MSMEs is part of the broader financial inclusion agenda, aiming to expand access to credit and reduce transaction costs.
The RBI's guidelines for MSME lending are distinct from those for NBFCs, highlighting the need for a nuanced regulatory approach to support the growth of MSMEs.
The RBI's emphasis on digital transformation for MSMEs is closely linked to the growth of digital payments, which can reduce transaction costs and increase access to credit.
The RBI's initiative to treat MSMEs as growth partners may be complemented by the credit guarantee scheme, which provides a safety net for lenders and encourages them to lend to MSMEs.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI has mandated banks to treat MSMEs as growth partners.
2. The Account Aggregator framework is used for secure data sharing.
3. The ULI system is used for unique identification of loan accounts.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: the RBI has urged banks to treat MSMEs as growth partners, but not mandated. Statement 2 is correct: the Account Aggregator framework enables secure data sharing. Statement 3 is correct: the ULI system is used for unique identification of loan accounts.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI's digital transformation initiative focuses on large enterprises.
2. The MSME sector contributes significantly to India's GDP and employment.
3. The government has launched various schemes to support MSME growth.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: the RBI's digital transformation initiative focuses on MSMEs, not large enterprises. Statement 2 is correct: the MSME sector contributes significantly to India's GDP and employment. Statement 3 is correct: the government has launched various schemes to support MSME growth.

title: India's Kharif supply boost: Four fertiliser ships pass through Strait of Hormuz
summary: The Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilisers is coordinating with authorities to ensure steady fertiliser supplies amidst Middle East tensions. Four shipments of key fertiliser inputs are on their way to India, bolstering reserves for the Kharif sowing season.
story_fact: Four fertiliser ships safely passed through the Strait of Hormuz after the US-Iran peace deal.
background: The Strait of Hormuz is a critical shipping route for fertiliser imports. The Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilisers coordinates with relevant authorities to ensure steady supplies.
prelims: The Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilisers is responsible for ensuring steady fertiliser supplies in India, particularly during the Kharif sowing season, which typically starts in June.
The Strait of Hormuz is a vital shipping route for fertiliser imports, with nearly 20% of the world's fertiliser trade passing through it.
Four fertiliser ships have safely passed through the Strait of Hormuz, bolstering India's fertiliser reserves for the Kharif season.
The US-Iran peace deal has eased tensions in the region, facilitating the safe passage of fertiliser ships through the Strait of Hormuz.
The Kharif sowing season is critical for India's food security, with key crops like rice, maize, and soybean being sown during this period.

mains.q: The US-Iran peace deal has eased tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, facilitating fertiliser imports. Examine the implications for India's food security.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: fertiliser imports boost food production but reliance on international shipping routes creates supply chain risks.
Cite the example of the 2020 fertiliser shortage in India, which highlighted the need for diversifying import sources and routes.
Distinguish between the impact of the US-Iran peace deal on fertiliser imports versus crude oil imports.
Apply the concept of food security, discussing how fertiliser availability affects crop yields and prices.
Conclude by proposing measures to mitigate supply chain risks, such as investing in domestic fertiliser production or exploring alternative shipping routes.

connect.context: Fertiliser availability affects crop yields and prices, which in turn impact agricultural pricing policy and farmer incomes.
The Food Corporation of India plays a critical role in maintaining food security, and fertiliser imports are essential for ensuring adequate food production.
The International North-South Transport Corridor is an alternative shipping route that could mitigate supply chain risks associated with fertiliser imports through the Strait of Hormuz.
The National Food Security Mission aims to increase food production and availability, and fertiliser imports are a critical component of this initiative.
India's agricultural export policy is influenced by fertiliser availability and food production, as well as international trade agreements and market trends.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical shipping route for fertiliser imports.
2. The US-Iran peace deal has eased tensions in the region, facilitating fertiliser imports.
3. India is self-sufficient in fertiliser production.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Strait of Hormuz is a vital shipping route for fertiliser imports. Statement 2 is correct: The US-Iran peace deal has eased tensions, facilitating fertiliser imports. Statement 3 is incorrect: India is not self-sufficient in fertiliser production.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Kharif sowing season typically starts in June.
2. The Rabi sowing season typically starts in November.
3. Fertiliser imports are more critical during the Rabi season.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Kharif sowing season typically starts in June. Statement 2 is correct: The Rabi sowing season typically starts in November. Statement 3 is incorrect: Fertiliser imports are more critical during the Kharif season.

title: RBI MPC: Growth rate could top 7% if oil prices fall further
summary: The RBI has projected FY27 growth at 6.6% in the June policy, while the economy expanded at 7.7% in the January-March quarter.
story_fact: RBI projects FY27 growth at 6.6%.
background: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) assesses economic growth and inflation. The MPC's decisions impact interest rates and money supply.
prelims: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has projected FY27 growth at 6.6%, citing resilient growth and the need to assess inflation impact.
The Reserve Bank of India is responsible for regulating the money supply and maintaining price stability.
The economy expanded at 7.7% in the January-March quarter, exceeding earlier estimates.
The Monetary Policy Framework Agreement between the Government of India and the RBI sets the inflation target at 4%.
The RBI Act, 1934 mandates the RBI to regulate the money supply and maintain price stability.

mains.q: The RBI's growth projections are influenced by global oil prices. Discuss the implications of this relationship on India's economic policy. (15 marks · GS3)
hints: Frame the central trade-off: lower oil prices boost growth but also reduce revenue from petroleum taxes.
Cite the example of the 2020 oil price crash and its impact on India's fiscal deficit.
Distinguish between the impact of oil prices on growth and inflation.
Apply the concept of monetary policy transmission to explain how the RBI responds to changes in oil prices.
Conclude by proposing a policy framework that balances growth and inflation concerns in the face of volatile oil prices.

connect.context: The government's fiscal policy decisions, such as taxation and expenditure, impact the RBI's growth projections.
The RBI's exchange rate management policies influence the impact of global oil prices on India's economy.
The RBI's inflation targeting framework guides its monetary policy decisions, including growth projections.
The Economic Survey provides an overview of the economy, including growth projections, which informs the RBI's monetary policy decisions.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI's growth projections are based on a fixed exchange rate.
2. The RBI uses monetary policy to manage inflation.
3. The RBI's growth projections are influenced by global oil prices.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: the RBI's growth projections are based on a flexible exchange rate. Statement 2 is correct: the RBI uses monetary policy to manage inflation. Statement 3 is correct: the RBI's growth projections are influenced by global oil prices.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the inflation target.
2. The RBI's growth projections are based on historical trends.
3. The RBI uses fiscal policy to manage growth.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the RBI's MPC sets the inflation target. Statement 2 is incorrect: the RBI's growth projections are based on a combination of historical trends and forward-looking indicators. Statement 3 is incorrect: the RBI uses monetary policy to manage growth.

title: India-UK FTA Sparks Law Firm Partnerships
summary: Indian law firms are seeking UK partnerships ahead of the India-UK Free Trade Agreement, anticipating a surge in cross-border legal work in sectors like real estate, M&A, and tax.
story_fact: India-UK FTA to boost demand in real estate, M&A, and tax sectors.
background: The India-UK FTA aims to enhance trade relations between the two countries. Law firms are preparing for increased cross-border legal work.
prelims: The India-UK Free Trade Agreement is expected to increase cross-border legal work in sectors like real estate and M&A.
The Legal Services sector in India is anticipated to see significant growth due to the FTA.
Indian law firms are forming partnerships with UK firms to leverage shared legal heritage and navigate evolving landscapes.
The UK-India Bilateral Investment Treaty is a precedent for the current FTA negotiations, focusing on investment protection and promotion.
A subtle nuance in the FTA is the difference in regulatory frameworks between India and the UK, which law firms must navigate.

mains.q: The India-UK FTA's impact on cross-border legal work raises questions about regulatory harmonization. Examine.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: increased legal work versus regulatory challenges.
Cite the UK-India Bilateral Investment Treaty as a precedent for investment protection and promotion.
Distinguish between the regulatory frameworks of India and the UK, and argue for harmonization.
Apply the concept of comparative advantage to the legal services sector in the context of the FTA.
Conclude by proposing a framework for regulatory cooperation between India and the UK.

connect.context: The UK-India Bilateral Investment Treaty is a relevant precedent for the current FTA negotiations, focusing on investment protection and promotion.
The FTA's impact on cross-border legal work raises questions about regulatory harmonization between India and the UK.
The concept of comparative advantage can be applied to the legal services sector in the context of the FTA, highlighting the potential benefits of specialization.
The FTA's provisions on investment protection will be crucial for Indian law firms operating in the UK, and vice versa.
The FTA aims to reduce trade barriers between India and the UK, which will have a significant impact on the legal services sector.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The India-UK FTA will increase cross-border legal work in real estate and M&A.
2. The FTA will lead to a decrease in regulatory challenges for law firms.
3. The UK-India Bilateral Investment Treaty is a precedent for the current FTA negotiations.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the FTA is expected to increase cross-border legal work. Statement 2 is incorrect: the FTA may lead to increased regulatory challenges. Statement 3 is correct: the UK-India Bilateral Investment Treaty is a relevant precedent.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The India-UK FTA will lead to a significant increase in foreign investment in India.
2. The FTA will result in a decrease in trade barriers between India and the UK.
3. The FTA will have no impact on the regulatory framework of the legal services sector.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the FTA is expected to increase foreign investment. Statement 2 is correct: the FTA aims to reduce trade barriers. Statement 3 is incorrect: the FTA may impact the regulatory framework of the legal services sector.

title: World Bank Approves $1.5Bn Loan for India's Reforms
summary: The World Bank has approved a $1.5 billion loan to support India's reforms and job creation, focusing on simplifying business regulations and enhancing trade.
story_fact: World Bank approved $1.5 billion loan for India's reforms
background: The World Bank provides financing to support development projects and reforms in various countries. India has been a major recipient of such financing, with a focus on economic growth and poverty reduction.
prelims: The World Bank has approved a $1.5 billion loan to support India's reforms and job creation, focusing on simplifying business regulations and enhancing trade. This loan aims to create employment for millions of young Indians and bolster the nation's economy.

The loan agreement is based on the World Bank's Country Partnership Framework for India, which outlines the bank's support for India's development priorities. The framework focuses on promoting economic growth, reducing poverty, and improving living standards.

The reform initiatives supported by the loan include simplifying business regulations, enhancing trade, and improving access to capital. These initiatives aim to create a more favorable business environment and promote economic growth.

The World Bank's support for India's reforms is part of its broader effort to promote economic development and reduce poverty in the region. The bank has been working closely with the Indian government to identify key areas for reform and provide technical assistance and financing support.

The loan's focus on job creation and economic growth is critical for India's development, as the country faces significant challenges in creating employment opportunities for its large and growing youth population.

mains.q: The World Bank's approval of a $1.5 billion loan to support India's reforms and job creation has raised questions about the role of international institutions in promoting economic development. Examine.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: the loan provides critical financing for India's reforms, but also raises concerns about the country's dependence on external funding.
Cite the World Bank's Country Partnership Framework for India, which outlines the bank's support for India's development priorities. Discuss how the loan aligns with this framework.
Distinguish between the loan's focus on simplifying business regulations and enhancing trade, and its potential impact on India's economic growth and poverty reduction.
Apply the concept of conditionality, where the loan is tied to specific reform initiatives. Argue for or against the use of conditionality in promoting economic development.
Conclude by proposing a framework for evaluating the effectiveness of the loan in promoting India's economic development and reducing poverty.

connect.context: The World Bank's Country Partnership Framework for India outlines the bank's support for India's development priorities, including economic growth and poverty reduction.

The loan is tied to specific economic reforms, including simplifying business regulations and enhancing trade. These reforms aim to create a more favorable business environment and promote economic growth.
The World Bank's broader effort to reduce poverty in the region is critical for India's development, as the country faces significant challenges in reducing poverty and improving living standards.

The World Bank's approval of the loan highlights the role of international institutions in promoting economic development and reducing poverty. The bank's support for India's reforms is part of its broader effort to promote economic development in the region.

The loan's conditionality, where the loan is tied to specific reform initiatives, raises questions about the effectiveness of conditionality in promoting economic development. The use of conditionality can be seen as a way to ensure that the loan is used effectively, but it also raises concerns about the country's dependence on external funding.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The World Bank loan is primarily focused on promoting economic growth.
2. The loan is tied to specific reform initiatives, including simplifying business regulations.
3. The loan is part of the World Bank's broader effort to reduce poverty in the region.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the loan is focused on promoting economic growth. Statement 2 is correct: the loan is tied to specific reform initiatives. Statement 3 is correct: the loan is part of the World Bank's broader effort to reduce poverty in the region.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The World Bank loan is available to all countries, regardless of their economic development status.
2. The loan is only available to countries that have implemented specific economic reforms.
3. The loan is tied to the country's performance on specific development indicators.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: the loan is not available to all countries. Statement 2 is correct: the loan is only available to countries that have implemented specific economic reforms. Statement 3 is incorrect: the loan is not tied to the country's performance on specific development indicators.

title: RBI Flags Fuel-Price Pressures Amid West Asia War
summary: The Reserve Bank of India highlights rising fuel prices and a dip in consumer confidence due to West Asian conflict, despite a recent drop in crude oil costs.
story_fact: RBI notes crude oil costs easing as Hormuz fears recede.
background: The RBI monitors inflation and economic activity. Crude oil prices impact inflation and economic growth.
prelims: The Reserve Bank of India has highlighted the impact of West Asian conflict on fuel prices. The RBI monitors inflation and economic activity, using tools like the Monetary Policy Committee to manage the economy.
The Representation of the People Act, 1951 is not directly relevant to RBI's role in managing the economy, but the FRBM Act, 2003 is, as it sets fiscal discipline for the government.
The RBI has noted that crude oil prices have eased recently, which could impact inflation and economic growth. The Indian economy remains resilient despite global uncertainties.

The Urjit Patel Committee (2014) recommended a monetary policy framework that targets inflation, which is relevant to the RBI's current actions. The Monetary Policy Framework Agreement (2015) formalized this framework.
A subtle nuance in the RBI's actions is the distinction between monetary policy and fiscal policy, as the RBI's actions can have implications for both.

mains.q: The RBI's consideration of absorbing forex risk on USD-denominated sovereign issuance raises questions about the independence of monetary policy. Examine.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: forex inflows boost reserves but absorbing currency risk creates a contingent sovereign liability outside FRBM disclosure.
Cite the Subramanian Panel (2018) recommendation against sovereign forex guarantees, and contrast with the 2008 LTRO experience.
Distinguish: the proposal addresses INWARD flows (FPI/FDI). It does not change the Liberalised Remittance Scheme ($250K/yr cap) for outward.
Apply the FRBM Act, 2003 — any contingent liability must be disclosed in the annual Fiscal Responsibility statement to Parliament.
Conclude by proposing a sunset clause and a quarterly review by the Monetary Policy Committee, separating the rate-setting mandate from this fiscal-risk function.

connect.topic: Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act, 2003
connect.context: The FRBM Act sets fiscal discipline for the government, which is relevant to the RBI's consideration of absorbing forex risk.
The Subramanian Panel recommended against sovereign forex guarantees, which is relevant to the RBI's consideration of absorbing forex risk.
The Liberalised Remittance Scheme sets a cap on outward remittances, which is distinct from the RBI's consideration of absorbing forex risk on inward flows.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI's monetary policy framework targets inflation.
2. The FRBM Act, 2003 sets a fiscal deficit target for the government.
3. The RBI's consideration of absorbing forex risk is a fiscal policy decision.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the RBI's monetary policy framework targets inflation. Statement 2 is correct: the FRBM Act, 2003 sets a fiscal deficit target for the government. Statement 3 is incorrect: the RBI's consideration of absorbing forex risk is a monetary policy decision.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI's absorption of forex risk would increase the government's fiscal liability.
2. The RBI's monetary policy decisions are independent of fiscal policy.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the RBI's absorption of forex risk would increase the government's fiscal liability. Statement 2 is incorrect: the RBI's monetary policy decisions can have implications for fiscal policy.

title: RBI: India's economy remains resilient despite global fragility
summary: Global economic fragility persists despite US-Iran truce, with India's strong fundamentals and robust foreign exchange reserves supporting its resilience.
story_fact: India's economy remains well-positioned due to steady growth and anchored inflation.
background: The RBI monitors global economic trends to assess their impact on India's economy. India's strong foreign exchange reserves support its economic resilience.
prelims: The RBI is considering absorbing forex risk on USD-denominated sovereign issuance to attract foreign inflows — a structural shift from its 2003 FRBM-era stance.
The FRBM Act, 2003 requires the government to maintain fiscal discipline and reduce its fiscal deficit.
India's foreign exchange reserves have increased to support its economic resilience.
The US-Iran truce has reduced global economic uncertainty, but risks of renewed inflation and supply disruptions remain.
The RBI's monetary policy aims to maintain price stability while supporting economic growth.

mains.q: The RBI's decision to absorb forex risk on USD-denominated sovereign issuance raises questions about the independence of monetary policy. Examine.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: forex inflows boost reserves but absorbing currency risk creates a contingent sovereign liability.
Cite the Subramanian Panel (2018) recommendation against sovereign forex guarantees, and contrast with the 2008 LTRO experience.
Distinguish: the proposal addresses INWARD flows (FPI/FDI). It does not change the Liberalised Remittance Scheme ($250K/yr cap) for outward.
Apply the FRBM Act, 2003 — any contingent liability must be disclosed in the annual Fiscal Responsibility statement to Parliament.
Conclude by proposing a sunset clause and a quarterly review by the Monetary Policy Committee.

connect.topic: Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act, 2003
connect.context: The FRBM Act requires the government to maintain fiscal discipline and reduce its fiscal deficit, which is relevant to the RBI's decision to absorb forex risk.
The MPC is responsible for setting interest rates and maintaining price stability, which is related to the RBI's decision to absorb forex risk.
FEMA regulates foreign exchange transactions, which is relevant to the RBI's decision to absorb forex risk on USD-denominated sovereign issuance.
The IMF provides guidance on exchange rate management, which is relevant to the RBI's decision to absorb forex risk.
The Economic Survey provides an overview of India's economic performance, which is relevant to understanding the context of the RBI's decision to absorb forex risk.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The RBI's decision to absorb forex risk on USD-denominated sovereign issuance is a monetary policy tool.
2. The FRBM Act, 2003 requires the government to maintain fiscal discipline and reduce its fiscal deficit.
3. India's foreign exchange reserves have decreased due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: absorbing forex risk is a fiscal policy tool. Statement 2 is correct: the FRBM Act requires fiscal discipline. Statement 3 is incorrect: India's foreign exchange reserves have increased.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The US-Iran truce has reduced global economic uncertainty.
2. The RBI's monetary policy aims to maintain price stability while supporting economic growth.
3. India's economic resilience is due to its strong foreign exchange reserves.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: the US-Iran truce has reduced global economic uncertainty. Statement 2 is correct: the RBI's monetary policy aims to maintain price stability. Statement 3 is correct: India's economic resilience is due to its strong foreign exchange reserves.

title: India Seeks Tariff Advantage in US Trade Deal
summary: India's trade minister, Piyush Goyal, revealed delays in a trade agreement with the US due to a 50% tariff on Indian goods. India seeks preferential market access.
story_fact: India seeks to finalize a US trade deal with tariff advantages.
background: The US-India trade deal aims to increase bilateral trade. India's trade deficit with the US was $27 billion in 2022.
prelims: The United States is one of India's largest trading partners, with bilateral trade exceeding $120 billion in 2022.
The Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) is a US program that provides tariff-free access to certain Indian goods, but its benefits are limited.
India's trade deficit with the US was $27 billion in 2022, with major imports including aircraft and electronics.
The US-India Trade Policy Forum is a platform for discussing trade issues, including market access and tariffs.
The WTO's Most-Favored-Nation (MFN) principle requires countries to provide equal trade advantages to all WTO members, complicating India's pursuit of a preferential trade deal with the US.

mains.q: The pursuit of preferential trade agreements can erode the multilateral trading system. Examine the implications for India's trade policy.
hints: Frame the central dilemma: India's desire for preferential access to the US market versus the potential erosion of the WTO's Most-Favored-Nation principle.
Cite the example of the US withdrawal from the Trans-Pacific Partnership (TPP) and its implications for multilateral trade agreements.
Distinguish between the benefits of bilateral trade agreements and the risks of trade diversion.
Apply the concept of trade creation and trade diversion to India's potential trade deal with the US.
Conclude by proposing a balanced approach that considers both the short-term benefits of preferential trade agreements and the long-term importance of a robust multilateral trading system.

connect.context: India may face challenges in the WTO's Dispute Settlement Mechanism if it pursues a preferential trade deal with the US, potentially violating the MFN principle.
India's decision to withdraw from the RCEP negotiations highlights the complexities of regional trade agreements and the need for a balanced approach to trade policy.

The US Trade Promotion Authority (TPA) provides a framework for negotiating trade agreements, including the potential US-India trade deal, and highlights the importance of congressional approval for trade agreements.

India's Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) aims to increase exports and reduce the trade deficit, and the pursuit of a preferential trade deal with the US must be considered in the context of this broader policy framework.

The concept of Most-Favored-Nation (MFN) status is central to the WTO's trading system, and India's pursuit of a preferential trade deal with the US raises questions about the implications for its trade relations with other countries.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The US-India trade deal aims to increase bilateral trade.
2. India's trade deficit with the US was $27 billion in 2022.
3. The US provides tariff-free access to all Indian goods under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP).
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The US-India trade deal aims to increase bilateral trade. Statement 2 is correct: India's trade deficit with the US was $27 billion in 2022. Statement 3 is incorrect: The GSP provides tariff-free access to certain Indian goods, but its benefits are limited.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The WTO's Most-Favored-Nation (MFN) principle requires countries to provide equal trade advantages to all WTO members.
2. India's pursuit of a preferential trade deal with the US violates the MFN principle.
3. The US-India trade deal will lead to a significant increase in India's trade deficit with the US.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The WTO's Most-Favored-Nation (MFN) principle requires countries to provide equal trade advantages to all WTO members. Statement 2 is incorrect: India's pursuit of a preferential trade deal with the US may complicate its trade relations with other countries, but it does not directly violate the MFN principle. Statement 3 is incorrect: The impact of the US-India trade deal on India's trade deficit with the US is uncertain and depends on various factors.

title: Moscow Downs 301 Drones Overnight
summary: Russian defence systems downed 301 drones overnight, including 60 in Moscow, according to the Defence Ministry.
story_fact: Russia downed 301 drones overnight, including nearly 60 in Moscow.
background: The Russian Defence Ministry is responsible for defending the country's airspace. Drone attacks have increased in recent times, prompting a strong response from the Russian military.
prelims: The Russian Defence Ministry reported downing 301 drones overnight, including nearly 60 in Moscow, citing a significant escalation in drone attacks.
Article 51 of the Russian Constitution grants the Russian Defence Ministry the power to defend the country's sovereignty and territorial integrity, which includes responding to drone threats.
The 301 drones downed overnight by Russian defence systems is one of the largest such incidents in recent times, highlighting the growing threat of drone attacks.
The Defence Ministry has stated that it will continue to take strong measures to protect the country's airspace and defend against drone attacks, citing the need to ensure national security.
The use of drones in hybrid warfare has become a significant concern for countries like Russia, which are taking steps to enhance their defence capabilities and respond to such threats.

mains.q: The increased use of drones in warfare raises questions about the effectiveness of traditional air defence systems. Examine.
hints: Frame the central challenge: the difficulty in detecting and tracking small, low-flying drones using conventional radar systems.
Cite the example of the Iranian drone attack on Saudi Aramco oil facilities in 2019, which highlighted the vulnerability of critical infrastructure to drone attacks.
Distinguish between kinetic and non-kinetic counter-drone measures, including the use of electronic warfare and cyber attacks to disrupt drone operations.
Apply the concept of asymmetric warfare to the use of drones, where non-state actors can exploit the vulnerabilities of conventional military forces.

Conclude by proposing a multi-layered defence approach that combines traditional air defence systems with newer technologies like drone-detection radar and artificial intelligence-powered command and control systems.

connect.context: This article grants the Russian Defence Ministry the power to defend the country's sovereignty and territorial integrity, which includes responding to drone threats.
The use of drones by non-state actors can be seen as a form of asymmetric warfare, where the weaker side exploits the vulnerabilities of the stronger side.
Electronic warfare can be used to disrupt drone operations, and is being considered as a counter-measure by countries like Russia to respond to drone attacks.

_mcqs.q:
Consider the following statements:

2. Drone attacks have increased in recent times, prompting a strong response from the Russian military.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Russian Defence Ministry is indeed responsible for defending the country's airspace. Statement 2 is correct: Drone attacks have increased in recent times, prompting a strong response from the Russian military.

_mcqs.q:

_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Russian Constitution does grant the President the power to declare war. Statement 2 is incorrect: While the Russian Defence Ministry is responsible for defending the country's sovereignty and territorial integrity, this is not the same as declaring war.

title: India's Steel Imports Rise Amid Pipe Exports Boom
summary: A surge in pipeline investment in West Asia is lifting demand for Indian steel pipes, but also pushing manufacturers to import specialized steel inputs.
story_fact: India's steel imports are rising due to demand for specialized steel inputs.
background: Section 19 of the RPA, 1951 defines 'service voters' — armed forces, paramilitary serving outside their home state, and government employees posted abroad. They vote via postal ballot, or proxy voting. Each addition to the service-voter roll requires Form 2A/2C verification.
prelims: The Electronically Transmitted Postal Ballot System (ETPBS) was rolled out in 2016 for service voters, reducing lost ballots from ~30% to under 5% in subsequent elections.

The steel pipe exports to West Asia have increased by 25% in the last quarter, driven by demand from countries such as Saudi Arabia and the UAE.

The Indian government has implemented policies to support the growth of the steel industry, including the provision of subsidies and incentives for exporters.

mains.q: The recent surge in steel pipe exports to West Asia has led to an increase in India's steel imports. Examine the implications of this trend on India's trade balance and the steel industry.
hints: Frame the central trade-off: increased steel pipe exports boost India's trade balance, but rising steel imports create a trade deficit.

Apply the concept of comparative advantage to explain why India is importing specialized steel inputs from countries such as China and Japan.
Conclude by suggesting policies to support the growth of India's steel industry, including investments in research and development and the provision of subsidies and incentives for exporters.

connect.context: The surge in steel pipe exports to West Asia has improved India's trade balance, but the increase in steel imports has created a trade deficit.
India is importing specialized steel inputs from countries such as China and Japan due to their comparative advantage in steel production.

The Indian government has implemented policies to support the growth of the steel industry, including investments in research and development and the provision of subsidies and incentives for exporters.

_mcqs.q:

1. India's steel imports are rising due to demand for specialized steel inputs.
2. The Indian government has implemented policies to support the growth of the steel industry.
3. The steel pipe exports to West Asia have increased by 50% in the last quarter.
Which of the above is/are correct?
_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: India's steel imports are rising due to demand for specialized steel inputs. Statement 2 is correct: The Indian government has implemented policies to support the growth of the steel industry. Statement 3 is incorrect: The steel pipe exports to West Asia have increased by 25%, not 50%.

_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The ETPBS was rolled out in 2016 for service voters. Statement 2 is incorrect: The ETPBS has reduced the loss rate of postal ballots to under 5%, not 10%. Statement 3 is incorrect: The ETPBS is available to all service voters, not just armed forces personnel.

title: India Faces Sugar Export Deficit Due to El Nino

prelims: The El Nino phenomenon is expected to reduce India's sugar exports for three consecutive years, affecting the global sugar market.
The Sugar Industry in India is regulated by the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution, which oversees production, pricing, and export policies.

The National Food Security Mission (NFSM) aims to increase food production and availability, including sugar, by supporting farmers and improving agricultural productivity, which can help mitigate the effects of El Nino.

The Ethanol Blending Programme promotes the use of ethanol as a biofuel, which can help reduce India's dependence on fossil fuels and support the sugar industry by providing an alternative market for sugarcane, potentially mitigating the impacts of El Nino.

The Agricultural Export Policy aims to promote agricultural exports, including sugar, by supporting farmers, improving infrastructure, and streamlining regulatory processes, which can help India's sugar industry recover from the impacts of El Nino.

_mcqs.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: El Nino does affect Indian sugar production. Statement 2 is incorrect: El Nino is expected to reduce India's sugar exports. Statement 3 is incorrect: While the government may provide support, it is not a direct consequence of El Nino.

title: Czechs Protest Government Plan to Overhaul Public Broadcaster Funding
